nodes.label: Symmetric RTP (Comedia) NAT Traversal
nodes.rationale: Chosen so inbound media over a public SIP trunk behind NAT flows without STUN or ICE; combined with a NAT-public contact learned from the registrar's received=/rport= (RFC 3261 s18.2.1 / RFC 3581) and trunk-DID line matching. Verified end-to-end by a real call and packet capture.

nodes.label: Record-Route Echo Fix (RFC 3261 s12.1.1)
nodes.rationale: Responses previously omitted the request's Record-Route headers, leaving the peer's route set empty; ACK/BYE then went straight to our Contact from an un-primed far-end node, which restricted NATs drop — the confirmed root cause of ACK/BYE never arriving over the trunk.

nodes.label: Media-Inactivity Timeout
nodes.rationale: A connected call whose inbound RTP goes silent is torn down as a NAT-safe fallback for a far-end BYE that never reaches the in-dialog Contact. Configurable via SdkConfiguration.InboundMediaTimeout (0 disables).

nodes.label: Module Registry (IVoipClientModule)
nodes.rationale: Generic extension point so feature modules ship as separate packages (DI before AddCallora or client.Modules.Register). Replaced unimplemented module facades that threw ModuleFeatureUnavailableException on every call; those capabilities return as separate plugin packages built on the public media API. Modules become resolvable only after OnAttached completes.

nodes.label: Per-Call Media Tap Contract
nodes.rationale: Pinned as a public, tested contract: parallel IMediaReceiver fan-out, detach/dispose isolation, IMediaSender injection, format discovery via ICall.MediaParameters. FrameReceived runs synchronously on the media path; consumers must buffer.

nodes.label: PreferredAudioCodecs Codec Preference
nodes.rationale: Ordered audio codec preference by SDP encoding name drives offers, answers and the primary codec of RTP sessions consistently; unsupported names are ignored, no match falls back to SDK defaults. Driven by G.711 u-law passthrough interop with an AVM Fritz!Box AI-agent scenario.

nodes.label: RFC 3550 Jitter Estimator Fix
nodes.rationale: Arrival-time conversion to RTP units saturated on a double-to-uint overflow, so reported jitter converged to the 20 ms frame interval on a clean link instead of ~0; now computed in integer math truncated modulo 2^32. RTT from RTCP receiver reports (LSR/DLSR) now feeds the adaptive jitter buffer instead of reporting the static 60 ms default.

edges.source_location: 3.1.1: RTT now feeds the adaptive jitter buffer
3.1.1: RTCP LSR/DLSR RTT feeds the adaptive jitter buffer
